case: Stacked little-optimizations on Ponder agent_do for web tasks (Calculator + FB Marketplace)
approach: User wanted to stack small optimizations (no H100, no model swap) toward 10× cumulative speedup vs Day 1 baselines.

optimizations_shipped_today.name: auto-targetApp from task text + coord-scatter anti-loop
optimizations_shipped_today.what: Detects native macOS app names in the task; auto-sets opts.targetApp + flat:true
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Crop optimization automatic for known apps

optimizations_shipped_today.name: raise-then-recapture before crop
optimizations_shipped_today.what: Raises targetApp to front + sleep 60ms + recapture before cropping
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Fixes Z-order occlusion (was: false-positive DONE on Ponder UI showing in cropped region)

optimizations_shipped_today.name: bounds-validate clicks against targetApp window
optimizations_shipped_today.what: Refuses clicks whose translated coords fall outside the target app's window
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Prevents focus changes from OOB clicks

optimizations_shipped_today.name: 300px size threshold for crop
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Routes Calculator-class tiny UIs to uncropped (slow but accurate) path automatically

optimizations_shipped_today.name: Modal /ground/batch upscale param
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Infra for future experiments (not productionized; tested with no accuracy gain on tiny UIs)

optimizations_shipped_today.name: wait-spam anti-loop + Chrome/Safari/Firefox auto-detect
optimizations_shipped_today.what: Bail after 3 consecutive 'wait' actions; auto-crop browsers
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Saves ~5 dead-time steps on stalls; auto-crop unlocks 5× on browser tasks

optimizations_shipped_today.name: generalize wait-spam → no-op-spam (catches enter/escape too)
optimizations_shipped_today.what: Match 'wait' / 'press enter' / 'press escape' / 'key enter|escape' for spam detection
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Catches 'goal already landed but brain didn't notice' (enter-spam) class

optimizations_shipped_today.name: URL-surfaced brain prompt + strict verifier
optimizations_shipped_today.what: Brain sees `URL:` + `Title:` block when targetApp is a browser; verifier defaults to RETRY unless concrete proof of completion; URL/screenshot patterns required
optimizations_shipped_today.win: ✅ VERIFIED on real run: strict verifier REJECTED a false-positive DONE on /marketplace/you with URL-specific reason. Yesterday rubber-stamped same state.

optimizations_shipped_today.name: /browser/url title-only fallback
optimizations_shipped_today.what: When Chrome Automation perm missing, fall back to System Events to read window title (uses Accessibility perm we already have)
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Works without requiring user to grant additional Automation perm

optimizations_shipped_today.name: constructable-URL keyboard fast-path hint
optimizations_shipped_today.what: When on Facebook Marketplace, hint brain to use cmd+l → type marketplace/search?query=X → enter (3 keyboard actions, no grounding needed)

optimizations_shipped_today.name: sharper FAST-PATH hint with concrete full-URL example
optimizations_shipped_today.what: Replace 'type the URL <pattern> replacing <term>' with a literal worked-example URL (already substituted for the user's query). Adds explicit anti-pattern: 'DO NOT type just the search term alone — that goes to Google.'
optimizations_shipped_today.win: Small model copies worked examples reliably; doesn't reliably substitute placeholders

day_1_baseline.calculator: 240s (10 calls with recovery from embedded-screenshot decoy)

after_strict_verifier_url_aware.bulbasaur_run_2: verifier REJECTED with specific URL reason 'The current page is the Facebook Marketplace Buying section (facebook.com/marketplace/you), not a search results page for bulbasaur'
after_strict_verifier_url_aware.won_class: False-positive DONE on web tasks — ELIMINATED

after_fast_path_hint_v1.bulbasaur_run_3: brain used cmd+l fast-path, typed 'bulbasaur' alone, ended on google.com/search?q=bulbasaur (not FB Marketplace search)
after_fast_path_hint_v1.verdict: Partial win — brain followed hint structure but missed URL construction

speed_accuracy_matrix.calculator_cropped_230x408_upscaled: 5-15s / 1-2-of-6 / math wrong (model converges to default coord at high upscale)
speed_accuracy_matrix.ponder_agent_do_chrome_with_all_fixes: 31-44s for 3-step search task (with strict verifier catching false-positives correctly)

stacked_speedup_realized.false_positive_class_eliminated: infinite (was: rubber-stamped wrong state; now: strict-RETRY)
stacked_speedup_realized.tiny_ui_calculator: no speedup possible without model change (geometry tricks fail at sub-300px)

what_didnt_work: Padding crop (added adjacent-window distractors that pulled grounding off-target)
Upscale 2×/3×/4× on tiny UIs (model converges to default coords; out of training distribution)
Pattern-substitution hints to the small brain ('type the URL <pattern>' was misread — brain typed just the placeholder term)
key_architectural_insights: 1. Below ~300px window size, no geometry trick (crop, pad, upscale) recovers grounding precision. Only full screen works.
2. Above ~300px, cropping IS the win — 5× consistent on Chrome.
3. Verifier strictness matters more than raw speed. A wrong-fast result is worse than slow-correct because the orchestrator trusts the verifier and stops investigating.
4. URL/title is THE most important state signal for web tasks. Surfacing it into both brain prompt AND verifier prompt closed an entire class of false-positives in one commit.
5. Small models follow worked examples, not pattern fill-ins. Always show the FULL constructed output, not a template with placeholders.
next_iteration_candidates: Test 1b8cb02 (concrete-URL fast-path hint) end-to-end. Expected: brain types the FULL URL string verbatim, lands on /marketplace/search?query=bulbasaur, strict verifier VERIFIED.

Add a /browser/navigate bridge endpoint that takes a URL and uses AppleScript `set URL of active tab of front window` — gives the brain a 1-action navigate primitive that bypasses ALL keyboard steps. Would require ~30 LOC + Automation perm.
Add 'recent visited URLs' to the brain context (last 3 URLs seen) so it can detect circular navigation patterns.

Per-target-app PROMPT cache. The brain's task prompt is ~3000 tokens of repeated boilerplate (action vocabulary, scope rules, etc). Prompt caching at the llama-server level would save ~80% of the input tokens per step.
